title: HBR Ideacast — Strategy Summit 2026: Why AI Means Radical Change
description: Highlights from HBR Ideacast: Strategy Summit 2026: Why AI Means Radical Change

highlights.title: 30% Rule Explained
highlights.description: The 30% rule is a proportionality that says individuals and organizations need a minimum technology and change capability threshold to contribute to a future with data, algorithms, and AI.

highlights.title: AI Enables Scale
highlights.description: AI enables scale, speed, and scope, allowing companies to serve millions of people quickly and make decisions faster.

highlights.title: Successful AI Adoption
highlights.description: Companies like Moderna, Domino's Pizza, and Rakuten have successfully adopted AI, achieving significant results such as increased productivity and revenue growth.

highlights.title: AI Driven Sales
highlights.description: AI-driven sales, such as those seen on TikTok, can lead to extraordinary sales figures, but require companies to be structured to deliver on these sales, with data integration and inventory management being key.

title: The Peter Attia Drive — #384 - Special episode — Obicetrapib: The CETP inhibitor with cardiovascular benefits and potential Alzheimer's prevention
description: Highlights from The Peter Attia Drive: #384 - Special episode — Obicetrapib: The CETP inhibitor with cardiovascular benefits and potential Alzheimer's prevention

highlights.title: Obesetrapib Explained
highlights.description: The host explains what Obesetrapib is, how it works as a CETP inhibitor, and its potential implications for Alzheimer's disease.

highlights.title: Lipid Biology Connection
highlights.description: The host discusses how lipid biology intersects with Alzheimer's disease, especially in E4 carriers, and explains the story of CETP inhibitors.

highlights.title: ApoB Class Explained
highlights.description: The host explains the ApoB class of lipoproteins, how they cause atherosclerosis, and their relationship with VLDLs and LDLs.

highlights.title: Indirect RCT Process
highlights.description: The host explains the indirect reverse cholesterol transport process, where HDL exchanges cholesterol ester for triglycerides with LDL particles.

highlights.title: CETP Inhibitors History
highlights.description: The host discusses the fascinating history of CETP inhibitors, their initial development, and the flawed assumption that raising HDL cholesterol is always good.

title: Manager Tools — The Difference Between Line And Staff Leadership
description: Highlights from Manager Tools: The Difference Between Line And Staff Leadership

highlights.title: Line Staff Difference
highlights.description: The speakers discuss the difference between line and staff managers, and how line managers should prioritize demands from line leaders over staff leaders.

highlights.title: Staff Leadership Examples
highlights.description: The speakers provide examples of staff leadership roles, such as human resources, finance, and IT, and explain how these roles differ from line leadership roles.

highlights.title: Prioritizing Demands
highlights.description: The speakers discuss the importance of prioritizing demands from line leaders, and how to handle situations where a line leader tasks a manager with a request that may not be feasible.

highlights.title: Deprioritizing Staff Demands
highlights.description: The speakers explain why managers should deprioritize demands from staff organizations, and how this approach can help managers manage their workload and prioritize tasks effectively.

title: Refactoring Podcast — Leading Beyond the Framework 🧭 — with Richard Hughes-Jones
description: Highlights from Refactoring Podcast: Leading Beyond the Framework 🧭 — with Richard Hughes-Jones

highlights.title: Coaching Journey
highlights.description: The guest shares his experience of writing an article with a former coaching client, which led to a unique and honest account of their coaching journey.

highlights.title: Non Technical Advantage
highlights.description: The guest explains how not having a strong technical background can be an advantage in coaching tech leaders, as it allows him to focus on other aspects of their development.

highlights.title: Common Challenges
highlights.description: The guest discusses the common challenges faced by senior leaders in tech, including ambiguity, complexity, and uncertainty, which are exacerbated by the current pace of change in the industry.

highlights.title: Ambiguity and Uncertainty
highlights.description: The guest highlights the difficulty of dealing with ambiguity and uncertainty, and how it requires leaders to be comfortable with not having all the answers, which can be a challenging mindset to adopt.

highlights.title: Leadership Development
highlights.description: The guest explains how traditional leadership development frameworks can only take leaders so far, and that further growth requires a more nuanced understanding of oneself and the ability to navigate complex situations.

title: The Diary Of A CEO — Manipulation Expert: How To Influence Anyone & Make Them Do Exactly What You Want! - Chase Hughes
description: Highlights from The Diary Of A CEO: Manipulation Expert: How To Influence Anyone & Make Them Do Exactly What You Want! - Chase Hughes

highlights.title: Influencing Humans
highlights.description: The speaker discusses the importance of understanding how to influence human beings, which can be applied to various professions and aspects of life.

highlights.title: Guiding Decisions
highlights.description: The speaker talks about helping people understand how to guide human decision and have great conversations that are very influential.

highlights.title: PCP Model
highlights.description: The speaker explains the PCP model, a three-step cascade that happens inside the human brain when we get influenced, and its significance in understanding human behavior.

highlights.title: Shifting Perception
highlights.description: The speaker discusses the importance of shifting perception and how it can be done by acknowledging someone's point of view and then introducing a new one.

highlights.title: Calling Out Scripts
highlights.description: The speaker talks about the power of calling out scripts that run inside people's heads and how it can transform their behavior.

highlights.title: Hypnosis Example
highlights.description: The speaker shares an example of a stage hypnosis show where a person is made to do silly things, illustrating the power of context and perception.

highlights.title: Context and Perception
highlights.description: The speaker discusses how context and perception can be used to influence people's behavior and decision-making.

highlights.title: Shifting Context
highlights.description: The speaker talks about the importance of shifting context and how it can be done in various situations, such as in a conversation with a kid or in a business meeting.

highlights.title: Radicalization
highlights.description: The speaker discusses how modifying perception and context can be used to radicalize people and make them do things they wouldn't normally do.

highlights.title: Following Curiosity
highlights.description: The speaker talks about the importance of following one's curiosity and not being trapped by external pressure, and how this approach can lead to more open and meaningful conversations.
